## [00:00 - 01:51]

Okay, we just spent a lot of time on talking about prospecting. Where you put your dollars, what kind of test we can run, and as you evaluate your business where you're at, does something work? Implement strategy number three. It's something not working. We started from zero, implement strategy number one. This is applicable across all the businesses, whether you're testing or looking for more scale. But how much dollars should we be spending? So we came up with this great little tool for you to use as a guideline to make your decisions. On the left side, did the ad spend 1x AOV? We use our AOV as a benchmark to understand what dollars we can spend so it can be statistically relevant for you, the advertiser, to make a decision on whether to scale or to cut it. If it didn't, let it continue to spend AOV. End of discussion. If it did and got a purchase, awesome. Let it continue. If it then got a purchase and you let it continue to run, and you look at your metrics that are right before it, which is our cost per initiative checkout, and before that, our cost per add to cart. What did we talk about earlier? Our correlations. Pull up the correlation ducts. What numbers do you need to hit? All the way down to that final purchase to continue to let it run. If it did get a purchase but now we're continuing to let it scale, compare your cost per required, cost per initiative checkout, and cost per add to cart against all your success correlation metrics. Guess what? If that works, let it continue to spend the 2x, 3x, and 4x. If not, kill it now. Hopefully, with this guideline and a document that we're about to give you for take away, you'll be able to make educated informed decisions so that you can scale or kill what's not working. Good luck.
